success_surface: Effective sheet-to-sheet data transformation with value mapping, column filtering, and structural preservation through hybrid pandas/openpyxl usage
effective_behaviors: Verified sheet existence programmatically before attempting access
Used pandas for data transformation and openpyxl for workbook structure maintenance
Applied value mapping transformations using dictionary-based replace operations
Selected only required columns for output rather than copying entire dataset
Preserved existing worksheet structure by deleting only data rows below headers
Implemented verification step by reading back generated output for validation
Wrapped core logic in error handling with descriptive logging throughout
Saved to new output path without modifying source file in-place
evidence: Check if required sheets exist: if 'INPUT' not in wb.sheetnames: raise ValueError('sheet not found')
Load INPUT sheet data using pandas for easier manipulation: df_input = pd.read_excel(input_path, sheet_name='INPUT')
Apply the origin mapping transformation: df_input['ORIGIN'] = df_input['ORIGIN'].replace(origin_mapping)
Select only the columns needed for OUPUT sheet: cols_to_copy = ['ITEM', 'BRANDS', 'TYPE', 'ORIGIN']
Delete all rows starting from row 2 (keep header row at row 1): for i in range(max_row, 1, -1): ws_output.delete_rows(i)
Verification: Read back and display: wb_verify = load_workbook(output_path); df_verify = pd.read_excel(...)
Save the workbook to the new location: wb.save(output_path)

memory_items.title: Sheet Existence Verification
memory_items.lesson: Always verify target worksheets exist in the workbook before attempting to read or write to them
memory_items.generalization: Programmatically check wb.sheetnames contains required sheet names and raise clear errors if missing to prevent runtime failures

memory_items.title: Hybrid Library Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Combine pandas for data processing efficiency with openpyxl for structure/styling preservation
memory_items.generalization: Use pandas.read_excel for complex transformations and openpyxl.load_workbook for writing results while maintaining non-data elements

memory_items.title: Value Mapping Transformations
memory_items.lesson: Define transformation mappings explicitly as dictionaries before applying them to columns
memory_items.generalization: Create key-value mapping structures and apply via DataFrame.replace() rather than manual conditional logic for maintainability

memory_items.title: Output Column Selection
memory_items.lesson: Identify and extract only the columns needed for the target destination
memory_items.generalization: Filter dataframe to relevant columns before writing to avoid unnecessary data transfer and preserve intended structure

memory_items.title: Row Deletion Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When clearing data from a sheet, iterate backwards and preserve header rows
memory_items.generalization: Use reverse loop starting from max_row down to 2 when deleting rows to maintain header formatting while clearing old content

memory_items.title: Post-Writing Validation
memory_items.lesson: Always read back generated output to verify successful completion
memory_items.generalization: Reload the saved file and compare contents against expected values to catch any silent corruption or incomplete writes

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Sheet Existence Checks**: Always verify wb.sheetnames contains required sheet names before accessing to prevent AttributeError.
- **Hybrid Processing**: Use pandas for data transformation (mapping, filtering, replacing) then openpyxl for writing to maintain workbook structure.
- **Explicit Value Mappings**: Define transformation rules as dictionaries; apply via .replace() for cleaner and more maintainable code.
- **Targeted Column Extraction**: Select only columns required for output destination to minimize unnecessary data movement.
- **Safe Row Clearing**: When overwriting worksheet data, delete rows in reverse order from max_row to 2 to preserve header formatting.
- **Verification Loop**: Read back the saved output file immediately after writing to confirm data integrity before reporting success.
